chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Time Which, if a Person Reaches it, They Are Considered to Have Performed tamattu‘

text: 2770 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلْمَرْأَةُ تَجِيءُ مُتَمَتِّعَةً فَتَطْمَثُ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَطُوفَ بِالْبَيْتِ فَيَكُونُ طُهْرُهَا لَيْلَةَ عَرَفَةَ فَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «إِنْ كَانَتْ تَعْلَمُ أَنَّهَا تَطْهُرُ وَ تَطُوفُ بِالْبَيْتِ وَ تَحِلُّ مِنْ إِحْرَامِهَا وَ تَلْحَقُ اَلنَّاسَ بِمِنًى فَلْتَفْعَلْ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2770 - It is narrated from Abu Basir, who said: I said to Abu Abdullah (as):
"A woman performing tamattu‘ begins menstruating before she performs tawaf of the Kaaba, and her purification occurs on the night of Arafah. What should she do?"
Imam (as) said: "If she knows that she will become pure, perform tawaf of the Kaaba, exit ihram, and join the people in Mina, then she should do so."
